notes.anchor: your taking no delight in my recommendation
notes.note: Commendatio: the formal letter of recommendation by which a Roman patron placed a friend or client with a powerful man. Cicero had recommended Trebatius to Caesar, and is mock-jealous that Trebatius now enjoys Gaul on his own account.

notes.anchor: C. Matius --- the most charming and the most learned of men
notes.note: Gaius Matius (Calvena), a cultured Roman knight and devoted intimate of Caesar, later a friend Cicero valued highly (Fam. 11.27-28). Cicero counts Trebatius's new friendship with him the best thing he could bring home from Gaul.
